texto: That's because true travel, the kind with no predetermined end, is one of the most selfish endeavors we can possibly undertake-an act in which we focus solely on our own fulfillment, with little regard to those we leave behind. After all, we're the ones venturing out into the big crazy world, filling up journals, growing like weeds. And we have the gall to think they're just sitting at home, soaking in security and stability.It is only when we reopen these wrapped and ribboned boxes, upon our triumphant return home, that we discover nothing is the way we had left it before.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephanie Elizondo Griest: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “That's because true travel, the kind with no predetermined end, is one of the most selfish endeav…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, china, cuba pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e termino.
